response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with 15+ years of experience, but his profile is centered on web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than the deep, current C++ expertise this role requires. His C++ exposure is limited to a short stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and academic projects, and there is no evidence of MQTT, IEC 62443/SDL, message-broker architectures at industrial scale, or industrial-automation domain experience. While his low-latency DLang systems work and testing/craftsmanship background are transferable, the gap on core requirements is too wide for a C++-focused senior manager role.
strengths: Extensive overall engineering experience (15+ years) across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec) using async I/O, caching, and fibers
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project), versus the role's core C++/STL requirement
No demonstrated experience with IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial cybersecurity
No MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.), or automation-industry background

key_factors.factor: Strong distributed-systems and low-latency engineering background
